title: Besseya cinerea
taxonomy: Besseya cinerea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a type of besseya, a small genus of plants that are native to North America. Besseya cinerea is further classified as a member of the tribe Veroniceae, a group of plants that are characterized by their showy flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is also known to be a part of the order Lamiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Besseya cinerea is considered to be a rare and unique species within its genus.

intro: Besseya cinerea, also known as cinerea fringed beardtongue, is a perennial plant species native to North America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and characterized by its distinctive fringed leaves and purple flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Besseya cinerea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Besseya cinerea is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally consumed to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
